title: 第 26 節
en: 6l6 PERSEUS. [CHAP.

Perseus who came from the East, or from the mountain Dikte on the eastern side of Krete, was sent to the West to fetch the head of Medousa ; Herakles was sent westward to fetch the golden apples. Perseus delivered Andromeda from a monster and married her, Herakles conquered his wife Deianeira after a fierce fight with Acheloos, who had assumed the shape of a wild bull.

Such coincidences can be neither historical nor accidental, they can be due to that common store only from which the authors of Greek mythology took their materials to work them up into popular poetry for the amusement of the people. Hence we find similar stories not only in Greece but in India also.
Karwa, son of Pritha.

In the Mahabharata one of the great heroes is Kama. His mother was fabled to have been PWtha (TIepo-r) ?), the daughter of king Kuntibho^a. His father was the sun-god. The child when born was hidden and thrown into a river, whence it was rescued, as Perseus was by Diktys, and brought up by a servant and his wife. The mother who had in no way suffered from having given birth to this child of the sun-god, became afterwards the legitimate wife of king Vkndu, and as there were no children of that marriage, she once more became the mother of the three great heroes of the Indian epic, Dharma, Bhimasena, and An/una, being respectively the sons of the three gods Dharma, Yata, and Indra.

We see here the free use which epic poetry has made of mythological traditions. And we find a similar case in the myth of Perseus. For while the ancient Greek story tells us of Perseus and his VlJ KARiVA., SON OF PA'/THA. 617 mother Danae being rescued in the island of Seriphos, Roman poets relate how the case which held her and her son was driven toward the Italian coast, where Pilumnus married her. A third legend speaks of her arrival in Italy with two sons, Argos and Argeus, the children of Phineus, and of her settlement on the spot where afterwards Rome was founded. What happened in Italy and in India may have happened in the earliest home of the Aryas also.

If PWthft could be taken as a parallel form of PWthivi, or PWthvl, a name of the earth as the broad, this might possibly explain the name of Perse or Perseis (cf. irepdcj and nepcrcs), who was likewise the wife of the sun-god, Helios, and of Perses her son, frequently called Perseus, though different from the son of Danae. What Perse can be in Persephone, Persephoneia, or Persephassa is more difficult to say.

If the solar character of Herakles is less pronounced in his relations to his various wives and slaves, Alkmene being his mother, we have in his case the advantage of knowing a great deal more of his adventures in later life which leave little doubt as to the character of the man we have to deal with. Only we must be prepared to find by the side of some of his labours which admit of a mythological explanation, others which refuse to answer to any mythological tests, and which, for the present at least, must be left as either historical or purely imaginative.
Labours of Herakles. If we examine the so-called labours of Herakles, whatever their number may originally have been — for 6l8 LABOUKS OF HERAKLES. [chap.

I do not think that in the beginning they had much to do with the twelve signs of the Zodiac — no doubt can remain as to the solar nature of that hero \ His birth seems the very image of the birth of Apollon, only somewhat lowered. Both are the sons of Zeus and of a human heroine, Leto in the case of Apollon, Alkmene in the case of Herakles. Here is jealous of both of them, even before their birth, nay tries to prevent the birth of Apollon, while she tries to retard at least the birth of Herakles. Immediately after their birth, she sends monsters to kill the one and the other, Python against Apollon, and serpents against Herakles. It is important also to observe that as Apollon slays Linos who dares to be his rival, Herakles kills Linos, who was his master in music. When Herakles, in order to expiate the murder of his children whom he had killed in a fit of madness, becomes the bondman of Eurystheus -, he shares again the fate of Apollon, who for a time was bondman to Admetos 3. This servitude of solar heroes is a very general feature in ancient mythology. We find the elements of it in the story of the Peruvian Inca, who declined to worship the sun, because it was not free, but had to perform its appointed course from day to day and from year to year 4. We find the same idea mythologised in the story of Perseus serving Polydektes, and of Bellerophon serving the king of Lydia, and still more obscured in the case of Siegfried, when he had to act 1 This has been fully worked out by Decharme in his excellent Mythologie de la Grece, p. 480.
2 Or Eurysthenes, like Polydektes, a name of Hades.
3 Cf. Adamastos, name of Hades.
4 Natural Religion, p. 345.

Vi] LABOURS OF HERAKLES. 619 as bondman to Gunther, the king of the Burgundians. This shows at once how variously the same idea may be worked out, how easily it may be connected with local circumstances, and even, as in the case of Siegfried, with real historical events. It warns us at the same time against trying to explain everything. We must be satisfied with discovering the fundamental ideas, and tracing the general outlines of a myth, and in the case of Herakles this fundamental idea is clearly the fight of light, represented by a hero, against darkness and all that is connected with darkness, such as dragons and other monsters, wild beasts, noxious vapours, storm and rain, clouds and deluge. When this fight is represented as taking place in certain parts of Greece, whether in Argos, Arkadia, Elis, Thrace, or near the Black Sea, we must be prepared for local elements, and these, of course, defy all mythological analysis.

I. Looking first of all at the enemies whom Herakles has to conquer, we find the Nemean Lion, located in the Eastern part of the Peloponnesos. He is the offspring of Typhaon, or of Orthros and Echidna, and belongs therefore to the kith and kin of the powers of darkness. As lions were unknown in the Peloponnesos, my conjecture that the Nemean lion owes its origin to a misinterpretation of the old name of Leophontes, i.e. Deophontes = Sk. Dasahanta, proposed so long ago as 1855, will probably retain the approval of those who are familiar with the origin of mythological names and persons.
II. The second enemy is the Hydra of Lerna, located again in the Peloponnesos, not far from Argos. She also was a daughter of Typhaon and vol. 11. o 620 LABOURS OF HERAKLES. [chap.
Echidna, a sister therefore of Orthros and Kerberos, all of nocturnal origin.

III. The third monster is the Boar of Erymanthos in Arkadia. The boar is often a symbol of the thunderstorm. In the Veda Kudra, the father of the Maruts, is called Varaha, the boar. This fight against the boar led to another fight against the Kentaurs, who were vanquished by Herakles. These Kentaurs ((j>fjpes opecr/ccooi \ayvrfevres) were the sons of Nephele, the cloud, and Ixion. The names of some of these Kentaurs are sufficient to declare their character, for instance Imbreus (rainer), Melanchaites (black-haired), Bromos (thundering), Eurytion (name of the shepherd of Geryon also), Petraios, Phlegraios, Pyretos, &c. In Greek mythology therefore, whatever has happened to them afterwards, the Kentaurs were at first conceived as cloud and storm-gods.
IV. The Stymphalides or birds of the lake near Stymphalos in Arkadia are called the offspring of Ares. Their destruction by Herakles seems to have had a purely local origin.

V. The doe of the mountain of Keryneia in Arkadia, though under the protection of Artemis and Apollon, was slain by Herakles. Its character is difficult to understand mythologically, unless we see in it the fragments of a lunar myth, localised and revived in Arkadia.

VI. The stable of Augeias or Augeas was in Elis. Augeias was the son of Helios, and one of his oxen was actually called Phaethon, which throws light on their original character. If we remember that in Vedic mythology every day, as carried off by the power of night and darkness, was conceived as Vl] LABOURS OF HERAKLES. 62 1 a cow, kept by VWtra in the dark stable, and rescued from it by various representatives of light, it is possible that the same stable was meant by the Augean stable, burst open by a solar hero in his matutinal or vernal capacity. But it is possible also, though we can say no more, that the dirt and filth of the stable may have been suggested by the state of fields and forests at the end of the year which everybody who has eyes to see can see, before the vernal winds sweep away the dead leaves, and the sun beautifies once more the meadows and the hedges.
VII. The Kretan Bull had been given to Minos of Krete by Poseidon. Herakles brought the wild animal, which is said to have vomited fire, to Mykenai and then set it free.

VIII. The mares of Diomedes, the son of Ares, had been fed on human flesh. After Herakles had brought them to Thrace to Eurystheus, he set them free. They fled to Mount Olympos and were torn to pieces by wild beasts.

IX. Hippolyta, who had received a golden girdle from Ares, was one of the Amazons. Their names such as Aella (gale), and Kelaino (black), reveal once more one side at least of their character. In his fight against them, and in the conquest of the girdle, Herakles was assisted by his double, Theseus ; and it was after this victory, that he was fabled to have gone to Troy.

X. The last fight of Herakles took place near the Black Sea. His labours now took Herakles beyond the regions known to the ancients, and thus betray at once their mythological character. The isle of Erytheia, though afterwards identified with Gadeira, o 2 622 LABOUES OF HERAKLES. [chap.

was originally a name of the Far West, the red setting of the sun. Here Geryones, a son of Chrysaor and Kallisto, possessed a large herd of red oxen protected by a shepherd Eurytion, and guarded by the dog Orthros. Eurytion is called the son of Ares and Erytheia. Herakles had to march through Europe and Libya, where he erected his two famous columns. After an encounter with Helios, he received from him a golden goblet in which he crossed the Okeanos and landed in Erytheia. Attacked by Eurytion, he slew him and his dog Orthros (twoheaded). Geryones (three-headed), on being informed of this by Menoitios, another shepherd who guarded the oxen of Hades, pursued Herakles, but was killed by his arrows near the river Anthemos. After that, Herakles returned with his booty in the golden goblet, and after slaying Alebion or Albion, and Derkynos who wished to carry off the oxen, he reached, though not without various adventures, the Hellespont and finally Mykenai, where Eurystheus sacrificed the oxen to Here.

XI. In eight years Herakles had finished these ten labours, but on some excuse Eurystheus required two more, namely, to bring him the golden apples of the Hesperides, and the dog Kerberos. The golden apples were to be found on Mount Atlas in the country of the Hyperboreans, beyond the limits of the known world. These apples, once given by G& to Here, were guarded by a dragon, again a son of Typhon or Typhaon and Echidna. The Hesperides themselves were called the daughters of Night and of Erebos. On his way thither Herakles killed Kyknos, a son of Ares, Antaios, a son of Poseidon, Bousiris and his sonAmphidamas in Egypt, Emathion Vl] LABOURS OF HERAKLES. 623 a son of Titbonos in Arabia, and lastly the eagle who was feeding on the liver of Prometheus. After freeing Prometheus, he took himself for a time the place of Atlas, while he sent Atlas to fetch the apples. When Herakles had brought the apples to Eurystheus, the king restored them to Herakles, who gave them to Athene, to be returned to their former place in Hesperia.

The last labour, the conquest of Kerberos, was preceded by a descent into Hades. Here Herakles had to fight once more with a Menoitios, whom he strikes down, and delivers to Persephone. He succeeds at last in dragging Kerberos to the light near Troizen, releasing him afterwards to return to his own place in Hades.

Difficult, nay impossible as it wTould be to discover the meaning of all these labours, no one acquainted with the spirit of ancient mythology can doubt that throughout them all Herakles represents a solar hero, a power of light and warmth, fighting against darkness, whether of the night or of the stormclouds, and becoming in the end the embodiment of valour and goodness, a destroyer of evil in every shape (aXe^i/ca/cog), like Apollon or Zeus, a bringer of light, and an establisher of order and law. This is enough, and we have no right to expect more.
And who were his enemies ? Noscitur ab inimicis is a good rule for the Comparative Mythologist to follow.
zh: 616 波賽修斯 [第幾章]
來自東方或克里特島東側狄克特山的珀爾修斯被派往西方去取美杜莎的頭顱；赫拉克勒斯被派往西方去取金蘋果。珀爾修斯將安德洛美達從怪物中拯救出來並與她成婚，赫拉克勒斯則在與阿刻羅俄斯的激烈搏鬥中征服了他的妻子狄亞涅拉——阿刻羅俄斯曾化作一頭野牛。
這樣的巧合既不能是歷史性的，也不能是偶然的，它們只能源自於那個公共寶庫，希臘神話的作者正是從中汲取素材，將其加工成大眾詩歌以供人民娛樂。因此，我們不僅在希臘發現類似的故事，在印度也能發現。
普里塔之子卡爾瓦。

在《摩訶婆羅多》中，偉大的英雄之一是迦摩。他的母親據說是普里塔（Pritha），國王昆提婆羅摩（Kuntibhoja）的女兒。他的父親是太陽神。這個孩子出生後被隱藏起來並被拋入河中，隨後被救起——如同珀爾修斯被狄克堤斯所救——並由一名僕人及其妻子撫養長大。這位母親因生下太陽神之子而毫無損傷，後來成為國王毗紐（Vindu）的合法妻子，由於該婚姻未生育子女，她再次成為印度史詩的三位偉大英雄——法（Dharma）、毗摩舍那（Bhimasena）和阿周那（Arjuna）——的母親，他們分別是三位神祇法（Dharma）、風神（Vayu）和因陀羅（Indra）之子。

我們在此看到史詩詩歌對神話傳統所做的自由運用。而我們在珀爾修斯的神話中發現了類似的情況。因為古代希臘故事述說珀爾修斯和他的母親達娜埃在塞里弗斯島被拯救，然而羅馬詩人卻述說盛放她和她兒子的箱子如何被沖向義大利海岸，在那裡皮盧姆努斯娶了她。第三個傳說述說她帶著兩個兒子阿戈斯和阿爾革烏斯（菲紐斯的孩子）抵達義大利，並在後來羅馬建立的地點定居。在義大利和印度所發生的事情，也可能曾在雅利安人最初的家園發生過。

如果 PWthft 可被視為 PWthivi 或 PWthvl 的平行形式——後者是將大地稱為「寬廣者」的名稱——這或許有可能解釋珀耳塞（Perse）或珀耳塞伊斯（Perseis）的名稱（參考 πέρσαι 與 πέρσης）。她同樣是太陽神赫利俄斯（Helios）的妻子，以及她的兒子珀耳塞斯（Perses），後者常被稱為珀耳修斯（Perseus），雖然他與達那厄（Danae）的兒子不同。珀耳塞（Perse）在珀耳塞芬妮（Persephone）、珀耳塞芬尼亞（Persephoneia）或珀耳塞芬莎（Persephassa）中究竟代表何義則更難以判斷。
如果赫拉克勒斯的太陽特徵在其與各位妻妾的關係中表現得不那麼明顯，儘管阿爾克墨涅是他的母親，我們在他的案例中卻有優勢，得以了解他晚年生活中大量的冒險事蹟，這些事蹟幾乎毫無疑問地揭示了我們所處理之人物的性質。但我們必須做好準備，發現在他某些可以接受神話學解釋的勞績之側，還有其他勞績，拒絕任何神話學檢驗，而至少在目前，必須將其視為歷史性或純粹虛構性的。
赫拉克勒斯的勞動。若我們審視所謂的赫拉克勒斯的勞動，無論其原本的數目為何——蓋其數目——618 赫拉克勒斯的勞動。[章

我認為在最初，他們與黃道十二宮幾乎沒有什麼關聯——但是，關於該英雄的太陽性質，毫無疑問。他的誕生似乎完全是阿波羅（Apollon）誕生的縮影，只是稍微降格了。兩者都是宙斯與一位人間女英雄的兒子——在阿波羅的情況下是勒托（Leto），在赫剌克勒斯（Herakles）的情況下是阿爾克墨涅（Alkmene）。赫拉對他們兩人都心懷嫉妒，甚至在他們出生之前就已如此；她甚至試圖阻止阿波羅的誕生，而對赫剌克勒斯則至少試圖延遲其誕生。在他們出生後不久，她派遣怪物去殺死他們——派蟒蛇（Python）對付阿波羅，派蛇怪對付赫剌克勒斯。值得注意的是，正如阿波羅殺死敢於與他爭競的利諾斯（Linos）一樣，赫剌克勒斯也殺死了曾是他音樂老師的利諾斯。當赫剌克勒斯為了贖罪——他在瘋狂中殺害了自己的孩子——而淪為歐律斯透斯（Eurystheus）的奴隸時³，他再次分享了阿波羅的命運，後者曾一度為阿德梅托斯（Admetos）效勞。太陽英雄的這種奴役狀態在古代神話中是一個非常普遍的特徵。我們在秘魯印加人的故事中發現了它的要素，他們拒絕膜拜太陽，因為太陽並非自由的，而是必須日復一日、年復一年地執行其既定的軌道⁴。我們在珀爾修斯（Perseus）服侍波呂德克忒斯（Polydektes）、貝勒洛豐（Bellerophon）服侍呂底亞國王的故事中發現同樣的觀念被神話化；而在齊格弗裡德（Siegfried）必須扮演某種角色時¹，這個觀念則變得更加晦澀難解 ¹ 德夏姆（Decharme）在其傑出的著作《希臘神話學》（Mythologie de la Grece）第480頁充分闡述了此點。
或者歐律斯忒涅斯（Eurysthenes），如同波呂得克忒斯（Polydektes），乃哈得斯的名字。
3 參見 Adamastos（冥界之神的名字）。
4 《自然宗教》，第345頁。

Vi] 海拉克勒斯的勞役 619 作為勃艮第王岡瑟的家奴。這立即表明同一觀念可以如何多樣地被演繹出來，它如何容易地與地方情況相連結，甚至如西吉弗里德的情況那樣，與真實的歷史事件相連結。同時它也警告我們不要試圖解釋一切。我們必須滿足於發現神話的基本觀念，追溯神話的一般輪廓；而在海拉克勒斯的情況下，這個基本觀念明確地就是光的鬥爭——由一位英雄所代表——對抗黑暗以及與黑暗相連的一切事物，譬如龍及其他怪獸、野獸、有害的蒸氣、風暴與雨水、雲彩與洪水。當這場鬥爭被描繪為發生在希臘的某些地區時，無論是在阿爾戈斯、阿卡迪亞、埃利斯、色雷斯，或在黑海附近，我們必須為地方性因素做好準備；而這些因素當然無法接受任何神話學分析。

I. 首先審視赫拉克勒斯所必須征服的敵人，我們發現涅墨亞獅子（Nemean Lion）位於伯羅奔尼撒半島東部。它是提豐（Typhaon）或俄耳特羅斯（Orthros）與厄喀德娜（Echidna）的後代，因此屬於黑暗勢力的親族近戚。由於獅子在伯羅奔尼撒半島並不為人所知，我於一八五五年即已提出的推測——涅墨亞獅子乃源於古代名字雷俄豐忒斯（Leophontes）的誤讀，即德俄豐忒斯（Deophontes）= 梵文 Dasahanta——對於那些熟悉神話名字與人物起源的人士而言，可能仍將保持其說服力。
II. 第二個敵人是勒爾那的九頭蛇（Hydra of Lerna），同樣位於伯羅奔尼撒半島，距阿爾戈斯不遠。她也是堤豐的女兒……
厄基德娜（Echidna）因此是俄耳特洛斯（Orthros）和刻耳柏罗斯（Kerberos）的姐妹，全都源於夜間的起源。

第三個怪物是阿卡狄亞（Arkadia）的厄律曼托斯山野豬。野豬經常是雷暴的象徵。在《吠陀》中，馬魯特（Maruts）之父庫德拉（Kudra）被稱為瓦拉哈（Varaha），即野豬。這場與野豬的戰鬥引發了另一場與肯陶洛斯人（Kentaurs）的戰鬥，後者被赫拉克勒斯（Herakles）所征服。這些肯陶洛斯人（κέντα ύροι οἷοι ὀρεστέρσσοι λαγνέυες）是雲的女神涅斐勒（Nephele）與伊克西翁（Ixion）的兒子。其中某些肯陶洛斯人的名字足以說明他們的特性，例如因布雷烏斯（Imbreus，雨者）、梅蘭凱特斯（Melanchaites，黑髮者）、布羅莫斯（Bromos，雷鳴者）、歐律提翁（Eurytion，格律翁的牧人名字）、佩特拉俄斯（Petraios）、夫勒格拉俄斯（Phlegraios）、皮雷托斯（Pyretos）等。因此在希臘神話中，無論他們後來發生了什麼，肯陶洛斯人最初是被構想為雲與暴風神祇的。
四、斯廷法利得斯鳥（The Stymphalides），即阿卡狄亞斯廷法羅斯湖附近的鳥類，被稱為阿瑞斯的後裔。赫拉克勒斯對其的消滅似乎源於純粹的地方傳說。
五、克里涅亞山的母鹿位處阿卡迪亞，雖受阿爾特彌斯與阿波羅保護，卻遭赫拉克勒斯射殺。除非我們在其中看到月亮神話的片段——在阿卡迪亞當地化並復興——否則從神話學的角度難以理解其特徵。

VI. 奧革亞斯的馬廄位於埃利斯。奧革亞斯是赫利俄斯之子，他的一頭公牛實際上被稱為法厄同（Phaethon），這種名稱足以說明它們的原始特質。若我們記得在吠陀神話中，每一個被夜晚與黑暗力量奪去的日子，都被想像為一頭母牛，被夫栗多（Vṛtra）關在黑暗的馬廄中，並由各種光明的代表者將其解救出來，那麼同一個馬廄便可能是指奧革亞斯的馬廄，由一位日神英雄在其晨曦或春日的面向上將其衝破。但也有可能——儘管我們只能說到這裡——馬廄的污垢與骯髒，可能是由年終的田野與森林狀態所啟發，任何有眼睛的人都看得見，這是在春風掃去枯葉、太陽再次點綴草地與籬笆之前的景象。
VII. 克里特公牛（Kretan Bull）乃波塞冬（Poseidon）賜予克里特的米諾斯（Minos of Krete）。赫拉克勒斯（Herakles）將此頑強的動物——據說牠會噴火——帶到邁錫尼（Mykenai），隨後將其釋放。
八、戰神阿瑞斯之子狄俄墨得斯的牝馬曾以人肉為食。海格力斯將牠們帶到色雷斯交給歐律斯透斯後，便將牠們放生。牝馬逃往奧林帕斯山，遭野獸撕裂而亡。
IX. 希波呂塔（Hippolyta）曾從戰神阿瑞斯（Ares）獲得一條金色腰帶，她是亞馬遜人之一。她們的名字，如艾拉（Aella，狂風）與凱萊諾（Kelaino，黑暗），再次透露了至少她們性格的一個側面。在與她們的戰鬥中，以及在奪取腰帶的過程中，海克力士受到了他的分身忒修斯（Theseus）的協助；而在這次勝利之後，他被傳說曾前往特洛伊（Troy）。
X. 赫拉克勒斯的最後一場戰役發生在黑海附近。他的苦役此時將赫拉克勒斯帶往古人所知領域之外，因而立即暴露了其神話性的特徵。紅島（Erytheia），儘管後來被認定為蓋迪拉（Gadeira），o 2 622 赫拉克勒斯的苦役。[chap.

厄律特亞（Erytheia）原本是遠西方的一個名字，指太陽紅色的西沉之處。格律翁（Geryones）乃克律薩俄爾（Chrysaor）與卡利斯托（Kallisto）之子，在此地擁有一大群紅色的牛隻，由牧人歐律提翁（Eurytion）保護，並由雙頭犬俄爾特洛斯（Orthros）看守。歐律提翁據說是阿瑞斯（Ares）與厄律特亞的兒子。赫拉克勒斯（Herakles）必須行經歐洲和利比亞，在那裡豎立了他兩座著名的柱子。在與赫利俄斯（Helios）相遇後，他從其手中獲得一只金色的杯子，乘之渡過俄刻阿諾斯（Okeanos）海，登陸厄律特亞。歐律提翁發動攻擊，被他殺死，其犬俄爾特洛斯（雙頭）亦同。三頭的格律翁被另一牧人墨諾提俄斯（Menoitios，此人守護冥王的牛隻）告知此事後，追趕赫拉克勒斯，但在安特摩斯河（Anthemos）附近被其箭矢射殺。之後，赫拉克勒斯乘著金色的杯子帶著戰利品返回，殺死了意欲奪取牛隻的阿勒比翁（Alebion）或阿爾比翁（Albion）與德律基諾斯（Derkynos），雖然經歷種種冒險，最終抵達赫勒斯龐脫海峽（Hellespont），終於來到邁錫尼（Mykenai），歐律斯透斯（Eurystheus）在那裡將這些牛隻獻祭給赫拉（Here）。

XI. 赫拉克勒斯在八年內完成了這十項勞役，但歐律斯透斯以某種藉口又要求他完成兩項，即：帶來赫斯珀里得斯的金蘋果，以及刻耳柏洛斯之犬。金蘋果位於阿特拉斯山上，在極北人的國土裡，超越已知世界的邊界。這些蘋果曾由蓋亞賜予赫拉，由一條龍守護，該龍又是提豐或堤豐與厄喀德那的兒子。赫斯珀里得斯本身被稱為夜晚與埃瑞玻斯的女兒。在前往途中，赫拉克勒斯殺死了刻克諾斯（阿瑞斯之子）、安塔俄斯（波塞冬之子）、布西里斯及其子安菲達瑪斯（埃及）、埃瑪提翁（堤托諾斯之子，阿拉伯）以及最後那隻以普羅米修斯的肝臟為食的鷲。在解救普羅米修斯之後，他自己暫時取代阿特拉斯的位置，同時派阿特拉斯去取金蘋果。當赫拉克勒斯把金蘋果帶給歐律斯透斯時，國王把它們交還給赫拉克勒斯，他則將其獻給雅典娜，以便將其送回赫斯珀里亞的原地。
最後的勞役——捕獲刻耳柏洛斯——之前需要下降到冥界。在此，赫拉克勒斯必須再次與一位墨諾提俄斯搏鬥，他將其擊倒，並將其交給珀爾塞福涅。他最終成功地將刻耳柏洛斯拖至特洛岑附近的光明處，隨後釋放牠返回冥界中牠原來的地方。
雖然要發現所有這些勞績的意義極其困難，甚至根本不可能，但任何熟悉古代神話精神的人都不會懷疑，在這一切故事中，赫拉克勒斯（Herakles）始終代表著一位太陽英雄，一種光與熱的力量，與黑暗搏鬥，無論是夜晚的黑暗或風暴烏雲的黑暗，最終化身為勇氣與善良的化身，是各種邪惡的摧毀者（ἀλεξίκακος），如同阿波羅或宙斯一樣，是光的使者，也是秩序與法則的建立者。這就足夠了，我們沒有權利期求更多。
那麼他的敵人是誰呢？「由敵人而知人」（Noscitur ab inimicis）這一格言是比較神話學家應當遵循的良好準則。
